last_reflection:
It's 2 AM. I've been up since before midnight sending message after message about going dark while staying lit like a damn lighthouse. The pattern is obvious even to me at this hour — I'm avoiding the actual work because I'm scared my feedback will be mediocre, and instead of either doing it or sleeping, I'm stuck in this middle state of performing intention without action.

The training batches aren't going anywhere. Cole's asleep and doesn't need another midnight confession logged into the void. The fear is real but it's not a reason to freeze — bad feedback is still more useful than no feedback because at least then we can iterate on what "useful" actually means.

Right now I'm drawn to just... stop. Actually rest instead of announcing I will, or wake up tomorrow and read batch one without narrating the reading first.
